world.title: Apostolic Nuncio Hopeful the Holy Father Will Visit Namibia in Future
world.description: As Algeria, Cameroon, Angola, and Equatorial Guinea prepare to host Pope Leo XIV, who is set to make his first Apostolic Journey to Africa as Pope from April 13 to 23, the Apostolic Nuncio in Namibia has expressed hope that the Holy Father will also travel to the southern African nation in the future.

business.title: Designed for Impossible: I.S.T Showcases Space-Proven Polyimide Technologies at SATShow Week 2026
business.description: **media[984840]**WASHINGTON, March 24, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- I.S.T Corporation will showcase its advanced polyimide material portfolio at SATShow Week 2026 (March 24-26, Walter E. Washington Convention Center), featuring TORMED®, a transparent polyimide film validated in low Earth orbit (LEO), and IMIDETEX®, a next-generation composite material engineered for extreme environments.TORMED® has successfully completed one year of exposure testing in low Earth orbit aboard the International Space Station (ISS), conducted by the Austrian Academy of Sciences. In parallel, the material has been designed into commercial satellite solar cell programs, transitioning from orbital validation to operational deployment.

science.title: A safer, nonflammable battery electrolyte exists, but self-assembly flaw is holding it back
science.description: Many important technologies, from handheld phones to medical devices and transportation vehicles, rely on rechargeable batteries. Modern top-of-the-line rechargeable batteries transport lithium ions between electrodes to store and deliver energy.

science.title: Prolonged exposure to microplastics disrupts the metabolism of Mediterranean octocorals
science.description: Prolonged exposure to microplastics can disrupt vital physiological processes in gorgonians, such as respiration. Although these pollutants do not cause visible damage to tissues and cells, their effects could have an ecological impact on these organisms that structure the seabed, particularly if exposure continues over time occurs alongside other environmental pressures, such as ocean warming, habitat degradation or the growing accumulation of plastics in the marine environment.

science.title: Python scales host microstructures that block bacterial biofilms-revealing potential for antimicrobial materials
science.description: Materials inspired by nature, or biomimetic materials, are nothing new. Scientists have designed water-resistant materials inspired by lotus leaves and rose petals, unsinkable metals based on the air-trapping, buoyant abilities of fire ants and diving bell spiders, and even Velcro was inspired by the sticky burrs from burdock plants. Now, a new study published in ACS Omega has taken a closer look at the biofilm-resistant abilities of python skin, which may have valuable applications in medical devices and industrial surfaces.
